stem: In triangle PQR, angle Q is a right angle, the measure of angle R is 41°, and the length of segment QR is 18 units. If the area, in square units, of triangle PQR can be represented by the expression k·tan 41°, where k is a constant, what is the value of k?
rationale: In right triangle PQR with right angle at Q, side QR is adjacent to angle R, and side PQ is opposite angle R. So tan R = PQ/QR, giving PQ = QR · tan R = 18 tan 41°. The area equals (1/2) · QR · PQ = (1/2)(18)(18 tan 41°) = 162 tan 41°. Thus k = 162.
